questions.question: Which declaration creates an int array of size 5?
questions.explanation: Arrays are declared with type[], and new int[5] allocates space for 5 ints. The other options are invalid or create a size-1 array with a single element 5.

questions.question: Given int[] a = {1, 2, 3}; how do you access the last element?
questions.explanation: Valid indices are 0..a.length-1; the last element is at index a.length - 1.

questions.question: Which statement about size is correct?
options: C) Arrays use length; ArrayList uses size()
D) ArrayList has capacity() for element count
questions.explanation: Arrays expose a length field; ArrayList exposes a size() method. There is no length on ArrayList.

questions.question: What happens with: for (Integer x : list) { if (x % 2 == 0) list.remove(x); }
options: A) It safely removes all even numbers.
D) It silently skips some elements but succeeds.
questions.explanation: Modifying an ArrayList during an enhanced for triggers ConcurrentModificationException. Use an Iterator with remove() or list.removeIf(...).

questions.question: With ArrayList<Integer> list, what does list.add(5) do?
options: A) Compile error: must write new Integer(5).
B) Stores primitive int directly in the list.
C) Autoboxes to Integer(5) and adds it.
D) Converts list to int[] and appends 5.
questions.explanation: Autoboxing converts the primitive 5 to an Integer object automatically.

questions.question: Given int[] a = {1,2,3}; which statement aliases the same underlying array rather than copying it?
questions.explanation: Assigning b = a makes b refer to the same array. The others create or perform copies (clone/copyOf/System.arraycopy).

questions.question: What are default initial values for new int[3] and new String[3]?
options: A) Uninitialized; compile error on access.
questions.explanation: Numeric primitives default to 0; object references default to null.

questions.question: Which code correctly removes all even numbers from ArrayList<Integer> list?
options: A) for (int i = 0; i < list.size(); i++) if (list.get(i) % 2 == 0) list.remove(i);
B) for (Integer x : list) if (x % 2 == 0) list.remove(x);
questions.explanation: removeIf safely removes elements matching a predicate. A) skips some elements; B) throws ConcurrentModificationException; D) is not a valid API.

questions.question: Given int[][] m = new int[3][4]; which expression is the number of rows?
questions.explanation: For 2D arrays, outer length is the number of rows; inner length (m[0].length) is columns.

questions.question: How do you sort an ArrayList<String> names in ascending order?
questions.explanation: Collections.sort(list) sorts a List in natural order. Arrays.sort is for arrays, and names.sort() requires an argument or Java 8 method reference but is not used as shown.
